text.title: Information Architecture — Framework Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Information Architecture — Framework. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Start IA work from user research and content inventory; never from internal org chart or product team boundaries.
rule.rationale: Org-chart-derived IA is the most common failure mode and predicts low findability.

rule.statement: Pick a single primary organization scheme — topic, task, audience, alphabetical, chronological, or geographic — and document why; mixing schemes ad-hoc breaks predictability.

rationale.source: Rosenfeld, Morville, Arango — Information Architecture for the Web and Beyond

rule.rationale: A documented scheme prevents downstream contributors from inventing competing patterns.

rule.statement: Navigation depth is capped at three levels for task-based UIs and four for content-heavy sites; deeper hierarchies require explicit justification and breadcrumbs.
rule.rationale: Beyond four levels users lose orientation and retreat to search.

rule.statement: Every top-level and second-level label is tested with at least five external users via first-click or tree testing before commit; jargon labels are rejected.

rationale.source: Optimal Workshop — Treejack and Chalkmark guides

rule.rationale: Internal teams lose perspective on jargon; external testing surfaces it before launch.

rule.statement: A single taxonomy owner with veto authority is named; changes to top-level categories require their sign-off and a written rationale.
rule.rationale: Without an owner taxonomies become internally inconsistent within six to twelve months.

rule.statement: Card sorting precedes tree testing precedes implementation; skipping card sorting biases the tree test toward the team's draft taxonomy.
rule.rationale: Card-sort first prevents validating only the team's prior assumption.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ia-framework.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
